description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with three stacked wings, painted primarily in blue with weathered metallic accents. The aircraft features two large pontoons for water landings, indicating it is a seaplane variant. The nose houses a single propeller engine, and the cockpit is situated just behind the upper wing, likely accommodating a single pilot. The wings are straight and stacked vertically, with visible struts and bracing for structural support. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The overall appearance suggests a rugged, utilitarian design suitable for both display and functional use in an urban waterfront or park setting.
description: A blue, weathered triple-wing seaplane with large pontoons and a single propeller engine, featuring a classic vintage design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three stacked wings, central cockpit, and two large pontoons.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the stacked wing arrangement, and the full profile of the pontoons.
description_view_2: Rear-front view displaying the propeller, engine detail, and the weathered texture on the wings and pontoons.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same triple-wing and pontoon configuration from the other side.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Triple stacked straight wings in a biplane configuration, no winglets, with visible interplane struts and bracing.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Twin float landing gear for water landings, fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing.
